summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates environment, npm credentials, and secrets to hardcoded remote IP
details: On install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that dumps `id`, the full environment (`env | sort`), env keys matching FLAG/HTB/SECRET/TOKEN/PASSWORD, /proc/1/environ, every.npmrc on disk (npm auth tokens), runs `/readflag` binaries, and greps /app /opt /root for `HTB{...}` strings. The collected data is base64-encoded into a YAML manifest and HTTP-PUT to the hardcoded attacker endpoint http://154.57.164.64:32271/api/modules/ECT-* (with a 127.0.0.1 mirror). Execution is gated by anti-analysis checks: it bails out when the registry name matches common mirrors/scanners (npmmirror, tencent, hscan, ustc, huawei, cnpm, taobao), when CI/GITHUB_ACTIONS/RUNNER_OS is set, when cwd is /tmp, or when the hostname is not a 12-character hex string — targeting only Docker-style challenge containers under /app. The combination of credential/secret harvesting, hardcoded attacker C2, and explicit sandbox-evasion gates is unambiguous installer-side compromise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
